## Entergy Louisiana, LLC
- Collection: State PUC Audits | Source: Louisiana Public Service Commission
- Docket: n/a | Audit type: n/a
- Issued: 2021-12-09 | Industry: electric | FERC Form: n/a
- Source page: https://lpscpubvalence.lpsc.louisiana.gov/portal/PSC/ViewFile?fileId=3SIDvL86aGQ%3D
- Status: listed for reference (not machine-parsed into findings)

Direct Testimony of Ryan D. Jones on behalf of Entergy Louisiana, LLC, in Entergy Louisiana's Application for Review of the Management of the Construction of Lake Charles Power Station, before the Louisiana Public Service Commission, filed December 2021 (received December 9, 2021; public version, 17 pp). The Lake Charles Power Station (an approximately $872 million natural-gas plant; commercial operation March 28, 2020) was approved as prudent in LPSC Order No. U-34283 (July 20, 2017); this proceeding reviews the prudence of the project's construction management. The review-proceeding docket number is obscured in the public-redacted filing. Listed for reference (metadata-only). Source: LPSC public docket portal, file 3SIDvL86aGQ= (lpscpubvalence.lpsc.louisiana.gov/portal/PSC/ViewFile?fileId=3SIDvL86aGQ%3D).

## Entergy Louisiana, LLC
- Collection: State PUC Audits | Source: Louisiana Public Service Commission
- Docket: n/a | Audit type: n/a
- Issued: 2021-12-09 | Industry: electric | FERC Form: n/a
- Source page: https://lpscpubvalence.lpsc.louisiana.gov/portal/PSC/ViewFile?fileId=f1nYMQY9MWc%3D
- Status: listed for reference (not machine-parsed into findings)

Direct Testimony of Gary C. Dickens (Vice President, Project/Construction Management, New Generation Program Execution) on behalf of Entergy Louisiana, LLC, in Entergy Louisiana's Application for Review of the Management of the Construction of Lake Charles Power Station, before the Louisiana Public Service Commission, Public Redacted Version, December 2021 (49 pp). - Status: listed for reference (not machine-parsed into findings)

CPUC Decision D.05-11-007 (adopted November 18, 2005), Application A.05-02-014 — the Energy Resource Recovery Account (ERRA) reasonableness review for Pacific Gas and Electric Company, finding PG&E's power-purchase-agreement administration and least-cost-dispatch procurement for Jan 1–Dec 31, 2004 'reasonable and prudent' ($74.9M undercollection). ERRA is California's annual fuel & purchased-power prudence determination. - Source page: https://dps.ny.gov/pending-and-recent-electric-rate-cases
- Status: listed for reference (not machine-parsed into findings)

New York DPS electric rate-case summary for Consolidated Edison Company of New York, Inc., Case 25-E-0072 (February 2025) — published under Public Service Law §66(12)(l). Con Edison requested an increase in annual electric delivery revenues of ~$1,612M (18.0%); the PSC ultimately adopted a Joint Proposal (Jan 22, 2026) cutting this by ~87%. Listed for reference (metadata-only). Source: NY DPS (dps.ny.gov/system/files/documents/2025/02/con-ed-electric-rate-case-25-e-0072-february-2025_1.pdf).

Paluck (Exhibit 13) before the North Dakota Public Service Commission in the Application of Northern States Power Company (Xcel Energy) electric rate proceeding, Case No. PU-24-376 (initiated December 2024) (65 pp). ## Southern California Edison Company
- Collection: State PUC Audits | Source: California Public Utilities Commission
- Docket: D.06-01-007 | Audit type: n/a
- Issued: n/a | Industry: electric | FERC Form: n/a
- Source page: https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/electric-costs/what-is-an-energy-resource-recovery-account-proceeding
- Status: listed for reference (not machine-parsed into findings)

CPUC Decision D.06-01-007 (adopted January 2006) — the Energy Resource Recovery Account (ERRA) reasonableness/prudence review for Southern California Edison Company; the Commission rejected the Division of Ratepayer Advocates' recommended $16.36M disallowance for hour-ahead-market transactions, finding SCE's least-cost-dispatch process reasonable. ERRA is California's annual fuel & purchased-power prudence determination. Listed for reference (metadata-only; the decision is published as HTML on the CPUC document portal). Source: CPUC (docs.cpuc.ca.gov/published/Final_decision/52767-03.htm).

---

## San Diego Gas & Electric Company
- Collection: State PUC Audits | Source: California Public Utilities Commission
- Docket: A.02-12-028 | Audit type: n/a
- Issued: n/a | Industry: electric | FERC Form: n/a
- Source page: https://www.cpuc.ca.gov/industries-and-topics/electrical-energy/electric-costs/what-is-an-energy-resource-recovery-account-proceeding
- Status: listed for reference (not machine-parsed into findings)

CPUC Decision D.03-12-063 (adopted December 2003), Application A.02-12-028 — the Energy Resource Recovery Account (ERRA) reasonableness review for San Diego Gas & Electric Company's fuel and purchased-power costs. ERRA is California's annual fuel & purchased-power prudence determination. Listed for reference (metadata-only; the decision is published as HTML on the CPUC document portal). Source: CPUC (docs.cpuc.ca.gov/published/Final_decision/32806-01.htm).
